desc: San Cristóbal de las Casas is a cool-climate highland city with Indigenous culture, craft markets, and easy access to Chiapas canyon and waterfall day trips.
hls: Colonial center and highland Maya communities
Pleasant daytime temperatures most of year
Strong café scene for longer stays
Excellent base for Sumidero and El Chiflón excursions

todo.name: Santo Domingo + textile market
todo.desc: Visit church complex and surrounding artisan market stalls.

todo.name: Cañón del Sumidero boat tour
todo.desc: Take a canyon cruise from Chiapa de Corzo.

todo.name: San Juan Chamula community visit
todo.desc: Join a respectful guided visit to nearby Tzotzil communities.

todo.name: Na Bolom museum stop
todo.desc: Learn regional ethnography and conservation history.

todo.name: Andador Real de Guadalupe food walk
todo.desc: Sample tamales chiapanecos, cacao drinks, and local bakeries.

todo.name: El Arcotete or Huitepec trail
todo.desc: Add a short forest or limestone walk near town.

wifi.note: Strong in central hotels/cafés, weaker on outer hill roads.

prac.fltNote: Travel from UK/Ireland generally connects via Mexico City, Cancún, or US hubs then onward to Tuxtla; holiday periods (Semana Santa, Christmas) see bus and flight price spikes.

airports.name: Ángel Albino Corzo International Airport (Tuxtla Gutiérrez)

prac.currency: Mexico local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Evenings can feel cold year-round due to altitude; pack layers even in warmer months.
Road blockades and demonstrations occur periodically in Chiapas—check local transport advisories.
Some Indigenous community visits require local guides and photo restrictions; follow posted rules.

sweet: Nov-Mar for dry, sunny highland days and comfortable walking weather.
